---
title: "Getting Started with rsparrow"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting Started with rsparrow}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

# Introduction

rsparrow is an R package for executing and evaluating SPARROW (SPAtially Referenced
Regressions On Watershed attributes) models. It generates estimation results,
predictions, and diagnostic output for static (long-term mean annual) SPARROW
water-quality models.

Static SPARROW models provide reach-level predictions (and uncertainties) of the
long-term mean annual water-quality loads and flow-weighted concentrations. The
predictions include the shares of the load attributable to individual upstream
sources and predictions of the mass quantities of the total load and individual
sources that are delivered to downstream water bodies.

# Installation

Install rsparrow from CRAN:

```r
install.packages("rsparrow")
```

Or install the development version from GitHub:

```r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("Kyle-Hurley/rsparrow")
```

# Basic Usage

```r
library(rsparrow)

# Run a SPARROW model (requires a configured model directory)
model <- rsparrow_model(path_main = "path/to/model/", run_id = "myrun")

# View a summary
summary(model)

# Generate predictions
model <- predict(model)

# Scenario analysis: reduce a source by 50 percent
model <- rsparrow_scenario(
  model,
  source_changes = list(fertilizer = 0.5),
  scenario_name  = "reduce_fertilizer"
)
```

# Model Directory Setup

A SPARROW model requires a directory containing:

- `sparrow_control.R` — control settings script
- `parameters.csv` — parameter configuration
- `design_matrix.csv` — source-delivery design matrix
- `dataDictionary.csv` — variable metadata
- `data1.csv` — reach-level input data

See the package documentation (`?rsparrow_model`) and the USGS RSPARROW
documentation for detailed setup instructions.

# Reporting Bugs

Please report bugs and ask questions on the GitHub Issues page:
<https://github.com/Kyle-Hurley/rsparrow/issues>

# Citation

Gorman Sanisaca, Lillian, Hurley, Kyle P., and Alexander, Richard B. (2025)
RSPARROW 2.1.0: An R system for SPARROW modeling. U.S. Geological Survey.
DOI: https://doi.org/10.5066/P1Y6RUBU

# Disclaimer

This software has been approved for release by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS).
Although the software has been subjected to rigorous review, the USGS reserves the
right to update the software as needed pursuant to further analysis and review. No
warranty, expressed or implied, is made by the USGS or the U.S. Government as to
the functionality of the software and related material nor shall the fact of release
constitute any such warranty. Furthermore, the software is released on condition
that neither the USGS nor the U.S. Government shall be held liable for any damages
resulting from its authorized or unauthorized use.

Any use of trade, firm, or product names is for descriptive purposes only and does
not imply endorsement by the U.S. Government.
